message.content: Adversaries may exploit Image File Execution Options (IFEO) to establish persistence and elevate privileges by executing malicious content when specific applications are launched. IFEOs allow developers to attach debuggers to applications, which adversaries can manipulate via the Windows Registry or GFlags tool to prepend malicious debuggers to application names. This technique can also enable arbitrary programs to launch when specified applications exit unexpectedly. By modifying IFEO Registry keys, adversaries can execute backdoor programs with SYSTEM privileges, especially during login or Remote Desktop Protocol sessions. This method can also be used to impair defenses by disabling security applications through invalid debugger registrations. Notable malware like SDBbot and SUNBURST have utilized IFEO for persistence and to trigger further malicious activities. Mitigation strategies include monitoring and auditing Registry changes, particularly under the IFEO keys, employing application whitelisting, and using endpoint detection and response (EDR) tools to detect and block suspicious debugger attachments.
